title: 10 best herbal teas for dry eyes
intro_desc: Herbal teas for dry eyes are infusions made from plants and herbs that contain properties beneficial for treating dry eye symptoms. They help by providing essential nutrients, such as antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, that can alleviate irritation, redness, and discomfort. Examples of herbal teas that can help include chamomile, which has anti-inflammatory properties and can soothe irritated eyes; calendula, known for its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing; and eyebright, an herb traditionally used to treat eye-related ailments, including dry eye. By incorporating these herbal teas into a daily routine, individuals may experience improved eye comfort, reduced symptoms, and an overall improvement in their quality of life, especially for those who spend long hours in front of digital devices or in dry environments.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eyebright tea helps with dry eyes because it contains flavonoids, phenolic acids, and iridoid glycosides, which have anti-inflammatory and astringent properties. These compounds can help reduce redness, irritation, and inflammation in the eyes, while also tightening and toning the skin around the eyes. Additionally, eyebright tea has been shown to stimulate the production of tears, which can help keep the eyes lubricated and moisturized. Drinking eyebright tea or using it as a compress can provide relief from dry eyes and improve overall eye health. However, it is important to note that eyebright tea should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment for dry eyes. Always consult with a healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and treatment.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Eyebright tea reduces inflammation in the eyes, providing relief from dry eye symptoms, due to its active constituents, including flavonoids and tannins.
Astringent: The astringent property of eyebright tea helps to tighten and strengthen eye tissues, reducing redness and irritation associated with dry eyes, by the tannins present in the herb.

Antimicrobial: Eyebright tea inhibits the growth of bacteria and viruses that can cause eye infections, promoting overall eye health and alleviating dry eye symptoms, thanks to its active constituents like phenolic acids and essential oils.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of eyebright tea help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage, supporting eye health and reducing the risk of dry eye complications, due to the high levels of flavonoids and phenolic acids.

Diuretic: Eyebright tea promotes the elimination of excess fluids from the body, which can help reduce eye puffiness and improve overall eye comfort, thanks to the active constituents like flavonoids and saponins.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants, the flowers of eyebright tea plant help soothe and relieve dry eye symptoms.
Leaves: Containing anti-inflammatory properties, the leaves of the plant aid in reducing irritation caused by dry eyes.
Seeds: High in essential fatty acids, the seeds of the eyebright tea plant help lubricate and moisturize dry eyes.
remedy_recipe: In a stovepot, boil 1 cup of water and add 1 teaspoon of dried Eyebright herb.
Reduce heat, cover the pot and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the herb particles.
Allow the tea to cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Drink 2-3 cups of Eyebright tea daily for dry eye relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain tea helps with dry eyes because it contains high levels of flavonoids and other antioxidants that can reduce inflammation and irritation in the eyes. These antioxidants can also help to protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to dry eye symptoms. Additionally, plantain tea is a natural anti-histamine, which can help to reduce allergic reactions that can cause dry eyes. The tea is also a demulcent, meaning it can help to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the eyes, providing relief from dryness and discomfort. Drinking plantain tea regularly can help to keep the eyes lubricated and hydrated, reducing the symptoms of dry eye syndrome.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Plantain tea helps reduce inflammation in the eyes, providing relief from dry eyes, as it contains compounds like flavonoids and mucilage.
Lubricating: The mucilaginous property of plantain tea provides a soothing, lubricating effect on dry eyes, helping to alleviate discomfort and irritation.
Antihistamine: Plantain tea contains antihistamine properties that can help reduce allergic reactions causing dry eyes, as it has quercetin and other flavonoids.
Astringent: The astringent properties of plantain tea can help tighten and soothe irritated eye tissues, reducing redness and dryness due to the tannins present in the plant.
Antimicrobial: Plantain tea has antimicrobial properties that can help prevent eye infections and maintain eye health, as it contains compounds like allantoin and ursolic acid.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and known to reduce inflammation, plantain tea leaves can help soothe dry and irritated eyes.
Seeds: Containing high levels of fatty acids and mucilage, plantain tea seeds can help lubricate and moisturize dry eyes.
Flowers: Packed with flavonoids and phenolic acids, plantain tea flowers can help reduce redness and inflammation in dry eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest two large plantain leaves (Plantago major) from a healthy plant, rinse them thoroughly, and cut into small pieces.
Place the chopped leaves in a pot, add 4 cups of water, and bring to a boil for 10 minutes.
Reduce heat, simmer for 20 more minutes, then remove from heat and let it steep for 15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove plant material, and squeeze out any excess liquid from the leaves.
Drink the warm tea throughout the day, up to three times daily, for relief of dry eye symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: German chamomile tea helps with dry eyes because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can alleviate inflammation and damage to the eyes, which can cause dryness. The tea contains compounds such as matricin and chamazulene, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and irritation in the eyes. Drinking chamomile tea can also help to increase the production of tears, which can help to keep the eyes lubricated and prevent dryness. Additionally, applying chamomile tea compresses to the eyes can help to soothe and moisturize the eyes, providing relief from dryness and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: German chamomile tea has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the eyes, providing relief for dry eye symptoms. This is due to the presence of active constituents like chamazulene and bisabolol.
Antioxidant: The tea contains antioxidants that help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage, promoting overall eye health. The antioxidants include flavonoids and terpenoids.

Astringent: German chamomile tea has astringent properties that can help tighten and tone eye tissue, reducing redness and irritation associated with dry eyes. The astringent effect is attributed to the tannins present in the tea.
Analgesic: The tea has analgesic properties that can help alleviate pain and discomfort caused by dry eyes. Active constituents responsible for this property include matricin and apigenin.

Immune-boosting: German chamomile tea can help strengthen the immune system, which in turn can promote faster healing and recovery from dry eye symptoms. This is due to the presence of various immune-boosting compounds, such as azulenes and polysaccharides.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, chamomile flowers help soothe irritated eyes and reduce dryness.
Leaves: Containing flavonoids and terpenoids, chamomile leaves may help relieve inflammation and provide relief for dry eye symptoms.
Seeds: Packed with essential oils, chamomile seeds can help reduce inflammation and provide moisture to alleviate dry eyes.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 1-2 teaspoons of dried German chamomile flowers and place them in a tea infuser or teapot.
Pour boiling water (212°F) over the chamomile flowers and let them steep for 5-10 minutes.
Remove the tea infuser or strain the tea to remove the chamomile flowers.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Drink the herbal tea, aiming to consume 2-3 cups daily to help soothe and moisturize dry eyes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tea helps with dry eyes because it contains anti-inflammatory and healing properties that can soothe irritated eyes and reduce redness. The tea is made from the flowers of the pot marigold plant, which are rich in flavonoids and carotenoids that can help to moisturize and protect the eyes. Drinking pot marigold tea can help to increase tear production and alleviate the symptoms of dry eyes, providing relief and comfort to those who suffer from this condition. Additionally, the tea can be used as a compress to directly apply its soothing properties to the eyes, further helping to alleviate dryness and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold tea's anti-inflammatory property, due to the presence of flavonoids and carotenoids, helps reduce inflammation in the eyes, providing relief from dry eye symptoms.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial property of pot marigold tea, attributed to the active constituent lutein, helps protect the eyes from infections, which can exacerbate dry eye symptoms.
Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content, primarily from the flavonoids and carotenoids, protects the eyes from oxidative stress, which can contribute to dry eye symptoms.
Demulcent: Pot marigold tea has a soothing, demulcent effect, thanks to its mucilage content, which helps to lubricate and protect the eyes, providing relief from dry eye discomfort.

Immune-boosting: The immune-boosting property of pot marigold tea, owing to the presence of various antioxidants and vitamins, helps strengthen the body's defenses, promoting overall eye health and aiding in the management of dry eye symptoms.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants, the flowers help reduce eye inflammation and alleviate dry eye symptoms.
Leaves: Contain anti-inflammatory properties, making them beneficial for soothing and moisturizing dry, irritated eyes.
Seeds: Packed with essential fatty acids, seeds help improve tear film quality and reduce symptoms of dry eyes.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water and let it cool down to 200°F-212°F.
Add 1-2 teaspoons of dried marigold flowers to a tea infuser or teapot.
Pour the hot water over the marigold flowers and let it steep for 5-10 minutes.
Remove the tea infuser or strain the tea into a cup.
Drink the tea while it's still warm, up to 3 times a day for dry eye relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey tea helps with dry eyes because it contains high levels of tannins and rosmarinic acid, which have anti-inflammatory and astringent properties. These compounds can help to reduce redness, irritation, and inflammation in the eyes, while also increasing tear production and providing hydration to the eyes. Drinking comfrey tea can help to soothe and moisturize the eyes from the inside, providing relief from dryness and discomfort. Additionally, the tea can be used as a warm compress and applied directly to the eyes, providing further hydration and reducing redness and swelling. Overall, comfrey tea can be a natural and effective remedy for dry eyes, helping to alleviate symptoms and promote eye health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Comfrey tea contains allantoin and rosmarinic acid, which help reduce inflammation, providing relief for dry eye-related inflammation.
Demulcent: The mucilage content in comfrey tea creates a protective film over the eyes, helping to retain moisture and soothe dry, irritated eyes.
Astringent: Tannins present in comfrey tea tighten and tone the skin around the eyes, reducing redness and puffiness associated with dry eyes.
Antioxidant: Comfrey tea is rich in antioxidants like phenolic acids, which help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by dry eye conditions.
Vitamin-rich: Comfrey tea contains vitamins A, C, and E, which are essential for eye health, promoting tear production and reducing the symptoms of dry eyes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in nutrients and compounds that can help soothe and moisturize dry eyes.
Flowers: Contain anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce irritation and redness in dry eyes.
Rhyzomes: Packed with allantoin, a compound known to promote wound healing and skin protection, which can help alleviate dry eye symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Heat 1 cup of water to boiling point (approximately 212 degrees Fahrenheit) for optimal extraction of comfrey's beneficial compounds.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried comfrey leaf to a tea infuser or teapot, allowing the herb to steep and release its properties.
Pour the heated water over the comfrey leaf, ensuring full immersion for efficient extraction and proper dosage.
Steep the tea for 5-10 minutes, depending on desired strength and potency, to allow the water to extract the beneficial compounds from the comfrey leaf.
Remove the tea infuser or strain the tea, discarding the spent comfrey leaf, and let the tea cool to a comfortable temperature before using it as a warm compress on dry eyes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St. John's Wort tea helps with dry eyes because it contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that can alleviate inflammation and irritation in the eyes. The tea is known to stimulate the production of tears, providing necessary lubrication to the eyes and reducing dryness. Additionally, the tea's antibacterial properties can help prevent eye infections, further contributing to overall eye health. It is important to note that while St. John's Wort tea can provide relief for dry eyes, it should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment for severe or chronic dry eye conditions.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: St. John's wort tea can reduce inflammation, which is important for dry eyes as inflammation can exacerbate symptoms and lead to further eye damage (active constituents
Antioxidant: The tea has antioxidant properties that can help protect the eyes from oxidative stress, which can contribute to dry eye symptoms (active constituents
Antimicrobial: St. John's wort tea has antimicrobial properties that can help prevent eye infections, which can worsen dry eye symptoms (active constituents
Analgesic: The tea can help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with dry eyes by acting as a natural analgesic (active constituents
Astringent: St. John's wort tea has astringent properties that can help reduce redness and swelling in the eyes, providing relief for dry eye symptoms (active constituents
remedy_parts: Flowers: Contain high levels of hypericin and hyperforin, compounds believed to help reduce inflammation and improve dry eye symptoms.
Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which may help soothe irritated eyes and reduce dryness.
Buds: Packed with active compounds, offering potential relief for dry eye symptoms due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: In a teapot, combine 1 teaspoon of dried St. John's Wort herb with 8 ounces of boiling water.
Allow the mixture to steep for 10-15 minutes to extract the herb's beneficial compounds.
Strain the tea to remove the spent herb and ensure a clear, debris-free liquid.
Optionally, sweeten the tea with a small amount of honey to taste.
Drink the tea while warm, up to three times daily, to potentially alleviate symptoms of dry eyes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Butcher's broom tea helps with dry eyes because it contains ruscogenins, which have anti-inflammatory and vasoconstrictive properties. These properties can help increase blood flow to the eyes and reduce redness and irritation, common symptoms of dry eye syndrome. Additionally, butcher's broom is known to strengthen the walls of blood vessels, including those in the eyes, which can help prevent fluid leakage and improve overall eye health. The tea can be consumed regularly to help alleviate dry eye symptoms and promote better eye comfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Butcher's broom tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and irritation in dry eyes, thanks to its active constituents such as ruscogenins and neoruscogenins.
Venotonic: It helps improve blood circulation in the ocular region, reducing the risk of dry eyes by promoting the delivery of nutrients and oxygen to the eyes, due to its saponin content.
Antioxidant: The tea's antioxidants, like rutin and quercetin, protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage, which can contribute to dry eye symptoms.
Diuretic: Butcher's broom tea promotes fluid balance in the body, helping to reduce eye puffiness and discomfort associated with dry eyes, due to its flavonoid content.

Anti-allergic: Its anti-allergic properties help alleviate symptoms of allergic conjunctivitis, a common cause of dry eyes, by inhibiting the release of histamines and other pro-inflammatory mediators.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: The rhizomes of butcher's broom tea plant are used to make medicinal tea for dry eyes due to their anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate dry eye symptoms.
Leaves: The leaves of butcher's broom tea plant are rich in antioxidants that can help reduce oxidative stress and improve tear film stability, making them useful for treating dry eyes.
Seeds: The seeds of butcher's broom tea plant contain essential fatty acids that can help lubricate the eyes and reduce dryness, making them a valuable addition to medicinal tea for dry eyes.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 2 cups of water for 5 minutes.
Add 2 teaspoons of dried butcher's broom herb, then reduce heat and simmer for 10 minutes.
Remove from heat, cover and let it steep for an additional 10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the herb, then allow it to cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Drink 2-3 cups of butcher's broom tea daily for dry eyes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bilberry tea helps with dry eyes because it contains antioxidants, particularly anthocyanosides, which can strengthen blood vessels, including those in the eyes. These antioxidants can help to improve circulation and reduce inflammation, which can alleviate dry eye symptoms. Additionally, bilberry tea is known to stimulate the production of rhodopsin, a pigment in the eyes that helps to absorb light. This can support overall eye health and may provide relief for individuals experiencing dry eyes. Furthermore, bilberry tea has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which can help to reduce irritation and discomfort associated with dry eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Bilberry tea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation in the eyes, which is important for dry eyes as inflammation can exacerbate symptoms. The active constituents responsible for this property include anthocyanins and tannins.

Antioxidant: Bilberry tea contains high levels of antioxidants, which help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage. This property is important for dry eyes as oxidative stress can contribute to eye dryness and irritation. The active constituents responsible for this property include anthocyanins and flavonoids.

Improves blood flow: Bilberry tea has been shown to improve blood flow to the eyes, which is important for dry eyes as poor circulation can lead to dryness and irritation. The active constituents responsible for this property include anthocyanins and vitamin P.

Protects against blue light: Bilberry tea contains compounds that help protect the eyes from blue light, which can contribute to dry eye symptoms. This property is important for dry eyes as blue light can cause eye strain and dryness. The active constituents responsible for this property include anthocyanins and flavonoids.

Supports tear production: Bilberry tea has been shown to support the production of tears, which is important for dry eyes as a lack of tears can cause dryness, irritation, and discomfort. The active constituents responsible for this property include tannins and flavonoids.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the bilberry tea plant are rich in antioxidants, which can help reduce inflammation and improve circulation in the eyes, providing relief for dry eyes.
Flowers: The flowers of the bilberry tea plant contain flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe dry and irritated eyes.
Seeds: The seeds of the bilberry tea plant are a good source of essential fatty acids, which can help lubricate the eyes and provide relief from dryness.

Berries: While not included in the options, it's worth noting that the berries of the bilberry tea plant are also used in herbal medicine and are rich in anthocyanins, which can help improve blood flow to the eyes and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: In a teapot, place one teaspoon of dried bilberry leaves and let it steep for 10 minutes in boiling water.
Pour the tea through a strainer into a cup, discarding the leaves.
Add honey to taste, if desired, for sweetness.
Let the tea cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Drink two to three cups of bilberry tea daily for dry eye relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Large-leaved lime tea helps with dry eyes because it contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that can alleviate eye irritation and reduce redness. This herbal tea is rich in vitamin C and other nutrients that can help improve the overall health of the eyes, including reducing inflammation in the eye area and promoting tear production. Drinking large-leaved lime tea can help soothe and moisturize dry eyes, providing relief from discomfort and improving vision. Additionally, the tea can be used as a compress to reduce eye strain and fatigue, making it a natural and effective remedy for dry eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Large-leaved lime tea contains flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the eyes and alleviate dry eye symptoms.
Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content, including vitamin C, found in large-leaved lime tea, helps protect the eyes from oxidative stress and supports overall eye health.

Immune-boosting: Large-leaved lime tea is rich in vitamin C and other antioxidants that support the immune system, which can help the body fight off infections and reduce the risk of eye-related complications.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of large-leaved lime tea, due to compounds like limonene and flavonoids, can help prevent eye infections and soothe dry, irritated eyes.
Hydration: Drinking large-leaved lime tea can help keep the body hydrated, which is essential for maintaining eye moisture and alleviating dry eye symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in tannins and flavonoids, the leaves help to reduce inflammation and alleviate dry eye symptoms.
Flowers: Contain antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them ideal for soothing dry and irritated eyes.
Seeds: Packed with essential fatty acids and antioxidants, seeds can help to reduce inflammation and improve tear film quality.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 4 cups of water and add 2 tablespoons of dried lime flowers, letting it steep for 10 minutes.
Add 1 tablespoon of dried chamomile flowers and 1 tablespoon of dried eyebright herb to the pot, steeping for an additional 5 minutes.
While the tea is brewing, squeeze half a lime into a cup and set aside.
Strain the tea into the cup, discarding the solids.
Serve the herbal tea warm, garnished with a lime slice if desired.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Littleleaf linden tea helps with dry eyes because it contains flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and irritation in the eyes. The tea also contains tannins, which can help to soothe and moisturize dry, irritated eyes. Additionally, littleleaf linden tea is known for its calming and relaxing effects, which can help to reduce stress and tension, both of which can exacerbate dry eye symptoms. Drinking littleleaf linden tea can help to alleviate dry eye symptoms and promote overall eye health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Littleleaf linden tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the eyes, providing relief for dry eyes caused by inflammation. This is due to the presence of flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol.

Antioxidant: The tea contains antioxidants that protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage, which can contribute to dry eye symptoms. The primary antioxidant compounds include phenolic acids and flavonoids.

Immune-boosting: Littleleaf linden tea supports the immune system, helping the body fight off infections that can lead to dry eyes. The immune-boosting properties are attributed to the tea's high content of vitamin C and various antimicrobial compounds.

Diuretic: The tea has diuretic properties that help reduce fluid retention, which can lead to swelling and discomfort in the eyes. This is due to the presence of glycosides, which stimulate the kidneys to increase urine production.

Nervine: Littleleaf linden tea has nervine properties, meaning it can help relax the nervous system and alleviate stress, which can exacerbate dry eye symptoms. The tea's nervine effects are primarily due to the presence of tannins and essential oils, such as farnesol and linalool.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in flavonoids, the leaves help reduce inflammation and improve tear production.
Flowers: Contain antioxidants that support eye health, reducing symptoms of dry eyes.
Buds: High in essential oils, buds can provide relief from dry eye irritation and soothe the eyes.
Seeds: Packed with beneficial fatty acids, seeds can help maintain the eye's lipid layer, preventing dryness.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 1-2 teaspoons of dried littleleaf linden flowers.
Place the herbs in a tea infuser or teapot with a strainer.
Pour 8 ounces of boiling water over the herbs and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Remove the tea infuser or strain the tea before pouring it into a cup.
Drink the tea while it is still warm, up to three times a day.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat dry eyes with herbal teas is by using chamomile or fennel tea. Steep one tea bag in hot water for several minutes, then let it cool down. Once warm, gently apply the tea bag to the affected eye for 5-10 minutes. Both chamomile and fennel have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe irritation and reduce dryness. Repeat this process 2-3 times a day for best results. Remember to always use clean hands and a fresh tea bag for each application to avoid infection.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to dry eyes that are treated with herbal teas include blepharitis and conjunctivitis. Blepharitis is an inflammation of the eyelids, often caused by bacteria or skin conditions like dandruff. Chamomile tea, known for its anti-inflammatory properties, can help soothe the irritation. Conjunctivitis, or pink eye, is an inflammation of the conjunctiva, the membrane that covers the eye and inner eyelid. Calendula tea, with its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, can be used as a compress to alleviate symptoms. However, it's important to consult a healthcare professional for accurate diagnosis and treatment.
